---
source: EU General Data Protection Regulation (Regulation (EU) 2016/679)
license: Public regulatory text; summaries paraphrased for RFP evidence.
url_reference: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj
summary: Selected articles relevant to SaaS RFP security questionnaires.
---

# Article 5 Principles Relating to Processing of Personal Data
Approved Wording: Personal data is processed lawfully, fairly, transparently, for specified purposes, limited to what is necessary, accurate, kept no longer than necessary, and secured against unauthorized access or accidental loss.

# Article 6 Lawfulness of Processing
Approved Wording: Processing is performed under the customer's documented instructions as a processor under Article 28. SentinelAI does not rely on a separate Article 6 ground for processor activities.

# Article 28 Processor
Approved Wording: SentinelAI executes a Data Processing Addendum with each EU/UK customer, including the EU Standard Contractual Clauses where required. Subprocessor changes follow a 30-day prior notice and objection window.

# Article 30 Records of Processing Activities
Approved Wording: Records of processing are maintained for every product line and are available to customers under NDA upon request.

# Article 32 Security of Processing
Approved Wording: Technical and organizational measures include encryption at rest and in transit, RBAC, MFA, continuous monitoring, regular control testing, and resilience planning.

# Article 33 Notification of a Personal Data Breach to the Supervisory Authority
Approved Wording: Confirmed personal data breaches are reported to affected customers within 72 hours of confirmation so the controller can meet the supervisory authority notification timeline.

# Article 35 Data Protection Impact Assessment
Approved Wording: A DPIA is performed for any new high-risk processing activity. Templates and prior DPIA summaries are available to customers under NDA.

# Article 44 General Principle for Transfers
Approved Wording: International transfers rely on the EU Standard Contractual Clauses, the UK International Data Transfer Addendum, or an adequacy decision. Onward transfers require equivalent safeguards.

# Article 45 Adequacy Decisions
Approved Wording: Transfers to adequacy-decision jurisdictions are governed by the Commission decision and the customer's DPA appendix.

# Article 46 Appropriate Safeguards
Approved Wording: Where adequacy does not apply, SCCs or BCRs are used together with supplementary measures including encryption and access controls reviewed by EU counsel.
